claim: LLM CHESS tests 50+ models using an agentic framework where LLMs play as Black against Random Player or Komodo Dragon
quote: We rank over 50 open and closed source models by playing against a random opponent

claim: Only reasoning models achieve positive Elo in LLM CHESS; o3 low peaks at ~758 Elo
quote: even the strongest LLM in our study, o3 (low), peaks at an adjusted Elo of about 758

claim: LLM CHESS claims resistance to contamination due to combinatorial richness of chess
quote: the stochastic and dynamic nature of LLM CHESS uniquely reduces overfitting and memorization while preventing benchmark saturation

claim: Dubesor AI Chess Leaderboard tracks 284 AI models across 3980 matches as of June 21, 2026
quote: Performance analysis of 284 AI models in 3980 chess matches

claim: Dubesor uses Stockfish 17.1 at depth 18 for accuracy analysis with dynamic Elo per mode
quote: The initial Elo rating is determined by analyzing the first 10 games... using Stockfish 17.1 at depth 18

claim: Benedict Brady benchmark tests endgame win %, puzzle Elo, and full-game Elo; Gemini 3.1 Pro leads at 1920 Elo
quote: BayesElo analysis of the full 144 games estimates Gemini at 1920 Elo

claim: Chess-LLM-Benchmark gives DeepSeek R1 ~994 Glicko-2 rating with 80% legal move rate from 5 games
DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 32B has -273 Glicko-2 rating with 71.6% legal moves from 35 games
DeepSeek R1 achieved 22.58% win rate against random opponent with 18.63 mistakes per game
quote: Deepseek-R1 | 22.58% Wins | 19.35% Draws | 18.63 Mistakes | 4585 Tokens/move

claim: Mathieu Acher concluded DeepSeek R1 is worse than GPT-2 at chess with illegal move rates exceeding 10%
quote: DeepSeek-R1 is worse than GPT-2 in chess: less capable of playing legal moves and less capable of playing good moves

claim: ChessArena (ACL 2026) provides framework with Glicko-1 ranking and trained Qwen3-8B-Chess via SFT+RL
quote: ChessArena has been accepted by ACL 2026 as a main conference paper

claim: PGN2FEN benchmark: DeepSeek Reasoner 82% on short sequences, 6% on 61-80 moves; DeepSeek Chat 25% on short, 0% beyond 20 moves
quote: deepseek-reasoner | 82 | 22 | 7.5 | 9.5 | 3 | 6

claim: ChessQA evaluates across five categories: Structural, Motifs, Short Tactics, Position Judgment, Semantic
quote: five task categories (Structural, Motifs, Short Tactics, Position Judgment, and Semantic)

claim: Zugzwang finds LLMs fail at chess primarily due to knowledge access, not reasoning capacity
quote: LLMs fail at chess primarily due to knowledge access, not reasoning capacity (arXiv:2507.00726)

claim: Krafton Chess-R1 fine-tuned Qwen2.5-7B and Llama-3.1-8B for chess via RL
quote: RL Fine-Tuning for chess with Qwen2.5 and Llama3.1 base models

claim: GLM-5.2 leads open-source models with 91.2% GPQA Diamond and 99.2% AIME 2026
quote: GPQA-Diamond 91.2 | AIME 2026 99.2

claim: LLM-Modulo verifier-in-the-loop raised RedPajama 3B move accuracy from 1.2% to 21.2% and validity from 19.3% to 95.3%
quote: LLM-Modulo raises RedPajama 3B's best move accuracy from 1.2% to 21.2% and move generation validity from 19.3% to 95.3%

claim: Crystallized intelligence (memorization) dominates LLM chess; fluid intelligence (novel positions) causes collapse
quote: crystallized intelligence remains a dominant... when fluid intelligence is required performance collapses

claim: DeepSeek V4 Pro leads coding at 83.7% SWE-bench Verified, Qwen 3.5 leads scientific reasoning at 88.4% GPQA Diamond
quote: DeepSeek V4 Pro 83.7% SWE-bench Verified; Qwen 3.5 88.4% GPQA Diamond

claim: GLM-5.2 scores 91 on BenchLM.ai provisional leaderboard, ranking #4 of 124 models
quote: GLM-5.2: 91 Prov. #4 of 124

claim: Kimi K2.5 achieves 96.1% on AIME 2025, 87.6% GPQA Diamond, 76.8% SWE-bench Verified
quote: AIME 2025 96.1 | GPQA Diamond 87.6 | SWE-bench Verified 76.8

claim: LLM CHESS framework uses AG2 (Autogen) with tools: get_current_board, get_legal_moves, make_move
quote: LLM agent uses Autogen ConversableAgent with tools: get_current_board, get_legal_moves, make_move

claim: Komodo Dragon levels Elo = 125 x (level + 1), Level 1 = 250 Elo
quote: Formula: Elo = 125 x (level + 1)

claim: DeepSeek R1 achieves 79.8% AIME 2024, 97.3% MATH-500, 71.5% GPQA Diamond
quote: DeepSeek-R1 achieves 79.8% Pass@1 on AIME 2024, 97.3% on MATH-500, 71.5% on GPQA Diamond
